name: Suspicious Execution of Dump64
description: Detects suspicious use of dump64.exe, a memory dump tool from Microsoft Visual Studio. Attackers may rename tools (like procdump.exe) to "dump64.exe" and place them in Visual Studio folders to bypass defenses and create memory dumps for credential extraction. This rule focuses on command-line arguments "-ma" (full dump) and "accepteula" (likely non-interactive use), which are often associated with malicious activity.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the process creation event.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The Sysmon Event ID for Process Creation (1).

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the executed process, which should be 'dump64.exe'.

parameters.name: process_path
parameters.description: The full path to the dump64.exe executable.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line used to execute dump64.exe, including arguments like '-ma' and 'accepteula'.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which dump64.exe was executed.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The hostname where the event occurred.
